source.sourcePath: raw/sources/DHF (인허가)/라우터 (Archer C64)/10. Test Lab ISO 17025 Certificate/ISO_IEC 17025_Test laboratory_BACL.pdf

document.title: ISO/IEC 17025 认可证书 No. CNAS L2408
standards: ANSI C63.4 (2014) with FCC Method 47 CFR Part 15, Subpart B

sourceSummary.oneLine: Chunk lists CNAS ISO/IEC 17025 accredited EMC and radio-disturbance test scope items, standards, clauses, exclusions, and effective dates across pages 1123-1174.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Use as evidence of laboratory accreditation scope for specific EMC/radio disturbance test methods and standards; this chunk is not a product-specific test result or submission authorization.
keyPoints: The certificate repeatedly states that the Chinese accreditation scope is the definitive version.

Accredited scope includes ISM, lighting, cellular base station, residential/light-industrial, multimedia, UPS, household appliance, IT, UWB, industrial, railway, maritime, telecommunication network, and power communication product EMC tests.
Many entries have effective date 2023-04-06; some updated industrial and lighting standards show effective date 2023-09-18.
Several immunity tests exclude 3-phase equipment and some cellular base-station entries exclude equipment larger than 0.5 m x 0.5 m x 0.5 m.

entities.name: CNAS
entities.evidence: Certificate header states ISO/IEC 17025 认可证书 No. CNAS L2408.

entities.name: ETSI
entities.evidence: ETSI EN 301 489-50, ETSI EN 301 489-33, and ETSI EN 300 386 standards are listed for EMC testing.

entities.name: FCC
entities.evidence: FCC PART 18 and FCC Method 47 CFR Part 15, Subpart B are listed as methods for ISM and information technology equipment.

entities.name: ICES
entities.evidence: ICES-001, ICES-003, and ICES-005 entries are listed for ISM, information technology, and lighting equipment.

concepts.name: ISO/IEC 17025 laboratory accreditation scope
concepts.evidence: Each page is headed ISO/IEC 17025 认可证书 No. CNAS L2408 with columns for Test Object, Item/Parameter, Standard or Method, Note, and Effective Date.

concepts.name: Electromagnetic compatibility testing
concepts.evidence: The scope lists EMC emissions and immunity tests including conducted disturbance, radiated disturbance, electrostatic discharge, surge, electrical fast transients, and voltage dips.

concepts.name: Radio Equipment Directive EMC essential requirements
concepts.evidence: ETSI EN 301 489-50 and ETSI EN 301 489-33 are described as harmonised standards covering essential requirements of article 3.1(b) of Directive 2014/53/EU.

concepts.name: Three-phase equipment exclusion
concepts.evidence: Multiple surge, fast transient, voltage dips, and related immunity entries include the note Except for: 3-phase equipment.

evidence.claim: The document is an ISO/IEC 17025 accreditation certificate scope under CNAS certificate number L2408.
evidence.evidenceText: Header repeatedly states ISO/IEC 17025 认可证书 No. CNAS L2408 and the table columns include Test Object, Item/Parameter, Standard or Method, Note, Effective Date.

evidence.claim: The Chinese version of the accreditation scope is identified as definitive.
evidence.evidenceText: The header states: The scope of the accreditation in Chinese remains the definitive version.

evidence.claim: The accredited scope includes ISM radio-frequency disturbance testing against CISPR 11, EN 55011, FCC Part 18, ICES-001, and GB 4824-2019.
evidence.evidenceText: Industrial, scientific and medical equipment entries list GB 4824-2019, CISPR 11:2015+AMD1:2016+AMD2:2019, EN 55011, FCC PART 18, and ICES-001 Issue 5.

evidence.claim: The accredited scope includes lighting equipment disturbance voltage, radiated electromagnetic disturbance, and EMC immunity tests.
evidence.evidenceText: Electrical lighting and similar equipment entries list CISPR 15, EN IEC 55015, ICES-005, GB/T17743-2021, and EMC immunity requirements EN 61547, IEC 61547, EN IEC 61547:2023.

evidence.claim: Cellular communication base station EMC tests are listed under ETSI EN 301 489-50 V2.3.1 with emissions and immunity parameters.
evidence.evidenceText: EMC for Cellular Communication Base Station, repeater and ancillary equipment lists ETSI EN 301 489-50 V2.3.1 for emissions, flicker, harmonics, electrostatic discharge, RF field, surges, and voltage dips.

evidence.claim: Some cellular base station EMC scope entries exclude equipment larger than 0.5 m x 0.5 m x 0.5 m.
evidence.evidenceText: Cellular base station entries note Except for: size > 0.5m x 0.5m x 0.5m, with some also excluding 3-phase equipment.

evidence.claim: Residential, commercial, and light-industrial EMC immunity and emission standards are included.
evidence.evidenceText: Residential, commercial, light industrial environment entries list IEC 61000-6-1, EN IEC 61000-6-1, AS/NZS 61000.6.1, GB/T 17799.1-2017, EN IEC 61000-6-3, IEC 61000-6-3, and GB 17799.3-2012.

evidence.claim: Multimedia equipment EMC emission and immunity testing is covered by EN 55032/CISPR 32 and EN 55035/CISPR 35 families.
evidence.evidenceText: Multimedia devices entries list EN 55032, GB/T 9254.1, AS/NZS CISPR 32, CISPR 32, VCCI-CISPR 32, CISPR 35, GB/T 9254.2, and EN 55035 for conducted/radiated disturbance and immunity items.

evidence.claim: UPS EMC testing is included for conducted disturbance, radiated disturbance, ESD, radiated immunity, fast transients, surge, conducted disturbances, and power-frequency magnetic field.
evidence.evidenceText: Uninterruptible power systems entries cite BS EN IEC 62040-2:2018, IEC 62040-2:2016/ISH1:2018, AS 62040.2:2008, and AS IEC 62040.2:2019.

evidence.claim: Household appliances, electric tools, and similar apparatus EMC emission and immunity testing is included.
evidence.evidenceText: Household appliance entries cite EN55014-1, EN IEC 55014-1, CISPR 14-1, GB 4343.1-2018, CISPR 14-2, EN IEC 55014-2, and GB/T 4343.2-2020.

evidence.claim: Information technology equipment radio disturbance testing includes FCC Part 15 Subpart B and ICES-003 methods.
evidence.evidenceText: Information technology equipment entries list ANSI C63.4 (2014) with FCC Method 47 CFR Part 15, Subpart B 15.107 and 15.109, and ICES-003 Issue 7 for conducted and radiated disturbance.

evidence.claim: Ultra Wide Band EMC testing is listed under ETSI EN 301 489-33 V2.2.1 for emissions and immunity parameters.
evidence.evidenceText: Ultra Wide Band (UWB)(EMC) entries list ETSI EN 301 489-33 V2.2.1 for emissions, flicker, harmonics, ESD, RF field, fast transients, surges, RF common mode, and voltage dips.

evidence.claim: Industrial environment EMC emission and immunity testing includes IEC/EN/GB/AS-NZS 61000-6-4 and 61000-6-2 families.
Electrical measurement, control, and laboratory equipment EMC testing is included under EN IEC 61326-1 and EN IEC 61326-2 series standards.
evidence.evidenceText: Entries list EN IEC 61326-1:2021, EN IEC 61326-2-1:2021, EN IEC 61326-2-2:2021, EN IEC 61326-2-3:2021 and corresponding GB/T 18268 standards for emissions and immunity items.

evidence.claim: Railway, maritime, telecommunication network, and low-voltage power communication product EMC scopes are included.
evidence.evidenceText: Later entries cite IEC 62236/EN 50121 railway EMC, EN 60945/IEC 60945 maritime equipment, ETSI EN 300 386 telecommunication network equipment, and EN 50065-2-1/-2-2/-2-3 power communication immunity requirements.

reviewFlags.reason: This is chunk 16 of 18, so extracted facts are limited to pages and entries visible in this chunk.
The chunk is an accreditation scope table, not a product-specific Archer C64 test report; no product model claim is supported here.
Many entries list effective dates rather than the certificate issue date; documentDate is left blank because no certificate-level date is visible in this chunk.
